title: calappa schizophylla
intro: Calappa schizophylla, also known as the "Split Pore Gac" or " Asian Gac", is a type of gourd plant native to Southeast Asia, specifically the tropical regions of China, India, and Southeast Asia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and anti-cancer properties, which are attributed to its high content of beta-carotene, lycopene, and other carotenoids. The plant is believed to provide various health benefits, such as improving eyesight, boosting the immune system, and reducing the risk of certain cancers, as well as exhibiting anti-aging and anti-inflammatory effects. Herbal preparations of Calappa schizophylla typically include extracts, teas, and capsules, which can be consumed to harness its medicinal properties.
